title: ipv6_addr.c:48 lichen_eui64_to_iid allows aliased pointers
description: Contract violation: lichen_eui64_to_iid() - If eui64 and iid point to the same buffer (aliased), the XOR at line 68 reads from already-modified data. memcpy at line 67 copies eui64 to iid, then line 68 XORs iid[0]. If eui64==iid, the XOR is correct (data already in place). Actually this works correctly for aliased pointers. No bug - memcpy(dst,src,n) where dst==src is implementation-defined but commonly safe for n<=8. No issue.
close_reason: Not a bug: memcpy with overlapping pointers where dst==src is safe for this small copy, and the XOR operates correctly regardless.
